title: Shahpur Kandi Dam Set to Operationalize — Ravi River Water Diversion Imminent
detail: India's Shahpur Kandi Dam on the Punjab-J&K border — expected operational by March 31, 2026 — will divert Ravi River waters currently flowing into Pakistan to irrigate 37,000+ hectares in India. J&K Jal Shakti Minister Javed Ahmed Rana confirmed the deadline. The dam is part of India's strategy to maximize use of the Indus basin rivers following its suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty in April 2025. Experts describe it as 'part of a broader strategy to increase costs on Pakistan.' Pakistan had previously stated that any interruption of water flows 'would be considered acts of war.' Pakistan referred the IWT suspension to the Permanent Court of Arbitration; India formally rejected the tribunal's jurisdiction in February 2026.

media.caption: Shahpur Kandi Dam set to operationalize, Ravi River water diversion imminent

title: Analysts: Pakistan Faces Dual Nuclear Scrutiny After US Threat Assessment
detail: Following the ODNI 2026 Annual Threat Assessment's flagging of Pakistan's ballistic missile program as a potential US homeland threat, Indian and Western analysts published analysis examining Pakistan's simultaneous roles as a US-Iran mediator and a nuclear-armed state under increased scrutiny. Pakistan's new Army Rocket Force Command (ARFC, established post-Sindoor), first test of the Taimoor air-launched cruise missile (January 2026), and the forthcoming induction of Hangor-class submarines are cited as Pakistan recalibrating its military doctrine toward stand-off deterrence in response to India's S-400 air defense. Analysts noted the convergence of Pakistan's diplomatic activism and military buildup is creating a complex security environment.

media.caption: Pakistan faces dual nuclear scrutiny after US threat assessment
